_comment: Sizing and cost estimation heuristics for Solr → OpenSearch migration. Used by report.py and assess_sizing tool.

node_count.rule: OpenSearch needs ~2x Solr nodes minimum due to replica co-location ban
node_count.explanation: Solr allows primary + replica on the same node; OpenSearch forbids it. A 3-node Solr cluster with RF=2 needs at least 6 data nodes in OpenSearch. Minimum 3 for HA.
node_count.master_nodes: 3 dedicated master nodes for production (can be smaller instances)

storage.rule: 3x current index size for operational overhead

components.merge_overhead: 1x — Lucene segment merges need temp space

storage.disk_type: gp3 (AWS) for most workloads; io2 for high IOPS needs

memory.explanation: Beyond 32GB, JVM loses compressed ordinary object pointers (oops). The other 50% is for OS page cache (critical for search performance).
memory.solr_to_os: If Solr runs 8GB heap on 16GB RAM, OpenSearch should get the same or slightly more

search_heavy.recommendation: r6g/r7g instances (memory-optimized) — search benefits from large page cache
search_heavy.example: r6g.xlarge (4 vCPU, 32 GB RAM) for ~500 QPS per node

index_heavy.example: c6g.xlarge (4 vCPU, 8 GB RAM) for ~5000 docs/sec per node

balanced.example: m6g.xlarge (4 vCPU, 16 GB RAM) — good starting point

small_dev.note: Don't use t3.small — 2GB RAM is not enough for OpenSearch

small.criteria: < 5 collections, < 50 fields, no custom plugins, simple queries

medium.criteria: 5-20 collections, 50-200 fields, some custom analyzers, eDisMax queries

large.criteria: 20+ collections, 200+ fields, custom plugins, complex queries, multiple client apps

enterprise.criteria: 50+ collections, custom similarity, streaming expressions, CDCR, regulatory requirements

Breaking.note: Breaking incompatibilities require design decisions, not just code changes

Behavioral.note: Behavioral differences need testing and tuning, not necessarily code changes

Unsupported.note: Unsupported features need workaround design; some may be migration blockers

library_swap.note: Replace client library + update query construction + test

framework_migration.note: Spring Data Solr → spring-data-opensearch, Drupal module swap, etc.

custom_http.note: Update URL patterns and request/response format

relevance_tuning_effort.note: BM25 vs TF-IDF causes 30-40% ranking difference. Budget for judgment set creation, Quepid/RRE evaluation, boost tuning.

components.relevance_testing: 10% — judgment sets, A/B comparison, nDCG validation

infrastructure.rule: Expect 1.5-2.5x Solr infrastructure cost, depending on deployment model
infrastructure.aws_managed_premium: 20-30% premium over self-managed for operational convenience
factors: Node count increase (~2x due to replica placement)
Managed service pricing if using AWS OpenSearch Service

human_effort.rule: Base weeks * team velocity + incompatibility overhead + integration overhead + relevance tuning
formula_pseudocode: total_weeks = base + incompat + integration + relevance + testing

common_surprises: Analyzer chains produce different results (requires tuning)
Client library migration takes longer than estimated
Undocumented Solr customizations surface during testing
AWS managed service limitations block a feature
